full_text:
Project overview

Input files used:

Description: We are currently using the files of the Sciset project for development. Also we will be using data from the SPACE project.

In addition we are using a multiplex dataset from Oncosys-OVA samples generated by Lassi Paavolainen using whole tissue slides and a panel described below (see table of contect). The corresponding H&E images are also available.

Paths:

-Scicet data in adequate format: https://datacloud.helsinki.fi/index.php/f/39250200

-H&E images of samples analysed by Lassi team: https://datacloud.helsinki.fi/index.php/f/51785595

Results and analysis files:

Description: The results are mainly generated on the go. The most important part of the project is the development of the analysis pipeline/scripts.

Computers used: Currently the analysis is mainly performed in Annti’s Laptop, further we will use CSC cloud computers (cpu1).

Paths:

- Preliminarily results generated by Fernando in summer 2023 are in datacloud: https://datacloud.helsinki.fi/index.php/f/39250188

Developed code or analysis utils:

Github: https://github.com/farkkilab/dev-virtualTMAs

Additional analysis utils: No additional code or analysis utils.

Notes: Code is based on Fernando’s work started in 2023 and further developed by Antti from Jan 2025 onward.

Experiment plans:

Description: The project is mainly about computational analysis, no additional wet lab experiments are planned.

tCycif experiment plan: NA we are not currently creating a new dataset

Other: NA, no additional experiments are needed.

Notes: We are receiving pathologist’s annotations from Anni Virtanen.

Additional key relevant files:

List of 78 samples sent to Lassi Paavolainen: L:\ltdk_farkkila\Projects\Collaboration Projects handled by Saun\Paavolainen collaboration block selection.xls

Members of the project:

Antti Toivanen (2025-)

Pablo Siliceo (2025-)

Anni Virtanen (2023-)

Fernando Perez (2023-)

Anniina Farkkila (2023-)

Project log

15.01.2025

Annotations in H&E by Anni Virtanen

In Slack Anni Virtanen wrote:

I made test annotations on one of the images. 3xtumorcentric, 3xtumor-stroma interface, 3xstromacentric, with 1 mm diameter.

I made the annotations on Qpath, using an ometiff image. I then overlaid the same annotations on the mrxs version of the image, and there is indeed a small shift in coordinates.

I will send you Fernando the qpdata file, exported geojson annotations also separately and the image in both formats.

Above Anniina suggests 4x3types, In the meeting the plan was this:

"Cancer rich

Stromal rich

Lymphocyte immune rich

Cancer-stroma interface

For each type of core, Anni will pick two sets of three cores (total 6), in total 24 cores per whole H&E image."

The number of annotations per image does not have a big influence on the time that the annotation task takes. Let me know what you prefer, 4/type or 2x3/type. And if you feel that having a category with "immune cell high content" would be good as well.

She made an annotation of one sample from the Sciset project (Inga-Maria project published in Cancer cell) using Qupath (H144_iOme2_PE_II_HE). That annotation is here in One Drive: virtual_tma

- Pablo started to inspect how to merge the H&E annotations made by Anni with the tCycIF images

03.02.2025

H&E images from Lassi Paavolainen

Lassi analysed 78 of the Oncosys-OVA samples. We will be using those slides for the vTMA project.

They sectioned 3 slides per FFPE block.

The number 1 slide was stained for HE, the number 2 slide was stained for mIHC and the number 3 slide is a reserve slide, it’s still unstained.

For multiplex imaging (mIHC) the used the next panel (10 plus DAPI): DAPI,CD68,CD3,CD56,CD11b,PDGFRbeta,CD20,CD31,CD45,HLA-DR,Epithelial

The images are already registered, they are still going to do the segmentation and quantification of signal per cell.

He shared the H&E from adjacent slides for annotation by Anni Virtanen. These files are here in datacloud: https://datacloud.helsinki.fi/index.php/apps/files/?dir=/farkkila/Projects/Virtual_TMAs/OvCa_HE&fileid=51785595

We already shared the H&E files for annotation to Anni.

03.02.2025

Literature review by Antti

Antti has been conducting an exhaustive literature review of various virtual TMA -based projects here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1rOPYutC2G-ePQSKlJlmtpIyYbXUc-NM8MCopU288vA8/edit?tab=t.0

There were none that exactly matched the goals of this project. Most were using only one or a few markers per study. Studying the whole cell atlas at once was only done in a couple of papers.

11.02.2025

Fixing and improving code by Antti

Antti has been updating the code Fernando wrote in 2023. Antti corrected several bugs and improved the structure of some functions. This is the main script he is updating and improving: https://github.com/farkkilab/dev-virtualTMAs/blob/main/02_vTMA_stats/vTMA_sampling_parallele.ipynb

The script so far generates two sets of vTMA from WSI, with random and annotation-guided sampling, and quantifies the cell type proportions in the generated vTMAs and WSI. In addition it quantifies the proportions of different cell types in the proximity of each cell type. The quantifications are done with each combination of possible parameters to study the effects of each parameter. Currently the analyses are limited to: fold-change in vTMA cores compared to WSI in overall cell type proportions and proximity proportions based on the size of “proximal” area and vTMA cores.

18.02.2025

Merging H&E and tCycif by Pablo

Pablo has been using a tool for merging H&E with tCycif with one of his students in Mexico. This is the tool: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-023-43915-7 
So far, the results are looking good, but still much more refinement is needed.

Also they are exploring calculation of alpha diversity estimators in relation to the size of area sampled as in this publication: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1574954118302322

26.05.2025

Meeting with Lassi Pavolainen

We had a meeting with Lassi Pavolainen on Mon 26th of May. From Lassi side was Naipunya and Gantugs Atarsaikhan. The notes and agreements related to the meeting are in the file: Lassi Paavolainen, Anniina Färkkilä vTMA meeting memo.docx

30.05.2025

Major updates to project code on github

Merged main branch with development branch containing major updates worked on in the past few months. These include:

New folder structure based on lab guidelines (see ~/dev-virtualTMAs /02_vTMA_stats/README.md)

Separation of sampling and analysis into different notebooks. (vTMA_sampling_parallele.ipynb is now vTMA_sampling_only.ipynb and vTMA_analysis_only.ipynb

New analyses and plots (spatial correlations, RCNs, etc. )

Bug fixes

And much more.

Extensive parameter testing for core sampling in CSC

Antti has been running the analysis in the CSC-cpouta cloud computer gpu2. The main analysis run in the cloud computer are the:

In whole slides.

Calculation of spatial correlation

For the next cell types: Fibroblast, Epithelial, CD4.T.cells, CD8.T.cells, Bcell, IBA1.CD163.Macrophages, CD11c.myeloid.

For the next markers: SMA, CD8a, CK7, CD4, CD20

Calculation of cell proportions

Pairwise celltype-celltype proximal associations

RCNs

Random sampling and guided sampling of cores:

Calculation of cell proportions in the sampled cores

Pairwise celltype-celltype proximity associations in the cores

Calculation of RCNs using the sampled cores

Calculation of spatial correlation in the sampled cores for the same markers and celltypes as above.

The parameters for sampling are the next:

For random sampling:

For each of the images

Up to 10 cores

10 different random states

500microns and 750microns radius

For guided sampling:

For each image with ROI

Up to 5 cores

1 different random state (algorithm is fully deterministic for now)

500microns and 750 microns

For the runs are used samples from the sciset project (Launonen, I. M.,et al. (2024). Chemotherapy induces myeloid-driven spatially confined T cell exhaustion in ovarian cancer. Cancer Cell). As input are used the cell quantification tables generated by Inga-Maria. For annotations in the guide sampling are used annotations made by Fernando only for cancer rich areas.

The script for execution are in:

https://github.com/farkkilab/dev-virtualTMAs/tree/main/02_vTMA_stats/scripts/vTMA_sampling_only.ipynb

The main results are in: ~/dev-virtualTMAs/02_vTMA_stats/analysis_results/ (local only for now)

The results are then downloaded and plotted in personal laptop using the next scripts:

https://github.com/farkkilab/dev-virtualTMAs/tree/main/02_vTMA_stats/scripts/vTMA_analysis_only.ipynb

January 2026

The guided sampling logic was changed from a single deterministic sampling to 10 repeated random samplings from the annotated ROI for performance reasons.

The vTMA pipeline has reached a suitable state for Antti to finish his thesis. This state of the sampling code is archived under the /dev branch on Github https://github.com/farkkilab/dev-virtualTMAs/blob/dev/02_vTMA_stats/scripts/vTMA_sampling_only.ipynb

(Some plots in the thesis were from later code updates, found under subsequent entries.)

February 2026

Further development of the pipeline is ongoing to apply it on new research questions. A re-design of the guided sampling system has been written at the request of Ziqi and Pablo, to have more control over how many cores are sampled from which tissue areas. This feature and further development is found under the /TSI branch on Github https://github.com/farkkilab/dev-virtualTMAs/blob/TSI/02_vTMA_stats/scripts/vTMA_sampling_only.ipynb

The re-write of guided sampling has left some functions of the sampling pipeline broken under this branch. To be fixed in future updates.
